verify.title: Fix Verification: {status}
verify.subtitle: Comparing against previous analysis
verify.transient_text: One-time spike from an older commit. The metric has since returned to its baseline. Safe to accept.
verify.stabilized_text: An older commit shifted this metric to a new level and it stayed there. The statistical model absorbed the change, but the value differs from the original baseline. Consider whether this shift was intentional.
verify.active_text: This metric is still actively deviating in the latest data. Investigation recommended.

findings.title: Key Findings
findings.empty: No unusual activity detected. Your project's development patterns are within normal ranges.

changes.title: What Changed in Your Codebase
changes.detected: We've detected {n} change{s} that differ from your project's normal patterns. Each change shows what typically happens versus what we observed this time.
changes.accepted_hint: These were previously reviewed and marked as expected. Manage with evo accept --list

card.was_compared: {metric} was {value}, compared to the typical value of {median}.
card.technical_text: The {metric} for this change was {value}. Historically, similar changes had a value of {median}.
card.hint_thisrun: Dismiss now, flag again next analysis
card.after_step1: AI suggests fixes → apply the changes to your code
card.after_step2: Run evo analyze . --verify to re-analyze and compare against this run
card.after_step3: If the change was intentional, click Accept above to dismiss it

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.dedup_note: {a} and {b} are treated as related metrics
patterns.what_are_patterns_text: Patterns are recurring correlations between different areas of your project. When a change in one area (e.g. a deployment) consistently coincides with unusual behavior in another (e.g. code dispersion), we flag it so you can decide if it needs attention.
patterns.what_should_you_do_text: Focus on items marked {action_required} or {needs_attention} first. These indicate patterns that are most likely to affect code quality, stability, or security. Items marked {worth_monitoring} don't need immediate action but should be reviewed if they persist. {healthy_pattern} and {informational} confirm that things are working as expected.

risk_banner.critical_text: One or more patterns indicate serious risks that could affect code quality, stability, or security. Review the items below and address "Action Required" findings before your next release.
risk_banner.concern_text: Some patterns suggest developing issues that could grow into problems if left unaddressed. Review the items marked "Needs Attention" and plan follow-up actions.
risk_banner.watch_text: Several patterns are shifting outside normal ranges. None require immediate action, but monitoring these trends will help you catch issues early.
risk_banner.info_text: Patterns detected are informational or healthy. No issues require attention at this time.
risk_banner.positive_text: All detected patterns are healthy. Your project's development patterns are working well.

investigation.title: Next Steps
investigation.step1_text: Copy the prompt below and paste it into your AI assistant (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT). It will identify root causes and suggest fixes.
investigation.step2_text: Apply the suggested changes. If a deviation was intentional, click Accept on its card above instead.
investigation.step3_text: Run evo analyze . --verify to re-analyze and compare. A verification banner will show which deviations resolved, improved, or persist.
investigation.show_full_hint: Click "Show Full Prompt" to see the complete investigation prompt with evidence...

sources.title: What EE Can See
sources.signals_analyzed: {n} signal{s} analyzed — all within normal range
sources.token_set: Token set. This data is analyzed automatically when running via GitHub Action or GitLab CI.
sources.connected_no_data_default: No events found in the analysis window.
sources.ci_no_runs: No pipeline runs found in the analysis window.
sources.deployment_no_releases: No published releases found in the analysis window.
sources.security_clean: No security alerts found — your dependencies look clean.
sources.error_no_events: No error events found in the analysis window.
sources.testing_no_files: No test result files (JUnit XML) found.

adapters.title: Expand Your Coverage
adapters.intro: Evolution Engine has {n} universal patterns learned from 200+ open-source repositories. The more signal families you connect, the more cross-family patterns can be detected.
adapters.available_subtitle: Enable these adapters to unlock additional signal families and cross-family pattern detection.
adapters.step1: Set the environment variable shown on the adapter card. For example: export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)
adapters.step2: For file-based adapters (Testing, Coverage): generate reports in your project first. For example, pytest --junitxml=junit.xml or pytest --cov --cov-report=xml
adapters.step3: Run analysis: evo analyze . — new adapters are detected automatically
adapters.step4: Verify: evo sources to confirm which adapters are active

footer.ai_disclaimer: This investigation was generated with the assistance of artificial intelligence (Claude by Anthropic). AI-generated content should be reviewed by a developer before acting upon recommendations.

insights.files_touched_up: More files changed than usual in this commit.
insights.files_touched_down: Fewer files changed than usual in this commit.
insights.dispersion_up: Changes spread across unrelated areas of the codebase.
insights.dispersion_down: Changes concentrated in related areas of the codebase.
insights.change_locality_up: The changed files frequently change together, suggesting a focused modification.
insights.change_locality_down: The changed files don't usually change together, suggesting a cross-cutting modification.
insights.cochange_novelty_ratio_up: Many file pairings in this change haven't been seen together before.
insights.cochange_novelty_ratio_down: Unusually low novelty — changes are more repetitive than normal, touching only well-known file combinations.
insights.total_tests_up: More tests are running than usual.
insights.total_tests_down: Fewer tests are running than usual.
insights.suite_duration_up: The test suite is running slower than usual.
insights.suite_duration_down: The test suite is completing faster than usual.
insights.skip_rate_up: More tests are being skipped than usual.
insights.skip_rate_down: Fewer tests are being skipped than usual.
insights.max_depth_up: Dependency tree is deeper than usual.
insights.max_depth_down: Dependency tree is shallower than usual.
insights.type_count_up: More types in the API schema.
insights.type_count_down: Fewer types in the API schema.
insights.field_count_up: More fields in the API schema.
insights.field_count_down: Fewer fields in the API schema.
insights.schema_churn_up: API schema changed more than usual.
insights.release_cadence_hours_up: Longer time between releases than usual.
insights.release_cadence_hours_down: Shorter time between releases than usual.
insights.user_count_up: More users affected by this error than usual.
insights.user_count_down: Fewer users affected by this error.
insights.fixable_ratio_up: A higher proportion of vulnerabilities have available fixes.

pattern_risk.dispersion_up_impact: Changes are spreading across unrelated parts of the codebase. This makes reviews harder and increases the chance of unexpected side effects.
pattern_risk.dispersion_up_rec: Review recent PRs for scope creep. Consider breaking large changes into focused commits.
pattern_risk.dispersion_down_impact: Changes are staying focused in related areas. This is a healthy development pattern that makes code review more effective.
pattern_risk.dispersion_down_rec: No action needed. This is a positive trend.
pattern_risk.files_touched_up_impact: Commits are touching more files than usual, increasing review burden and risk of regressions.
pattern_risk.files_touched_up_rec: Monitor PR sizes. If this persists, investigate whether large refactors need better decomposition.
pattern_risk.files_touched_down_impact: Changes are smaller than usual. Smaller changes are easier to review and less risky.
pattern_risk.cochange_novelty_ratio_up_impact: Files that don't normally change together are being modified in the same commits. This indicates novel, untested interactions that may introduce bugs.
pattern_risk.cochange_novelty_ratio_up_rec: Investigate which files are being combined unexpectedly. Prioritize testing these changes.
pattern_risk.cochange_novelty_ratio_down_impact: File co-change novelty is unusually low. While familiar patterns reduce risk, this could indicate repetitive or mechanical changes rather than normal development.
pattern_risk.cochange_novelty_ratio_down_rec: Review whether recent commits are routine maintenance or a sign of narrow, repetitive modifications that lack expected variety.
pattern_risk.change_locality_up_impact: Changed files frequently change together, suggesting focused modifications to tightly-coupled components.
pattern_risk.change_locality_up_rec: No immediate action. Monitor if tight coupling becomes a maintenance concern.
pattern_risk.change_locality_down_impact: Changes span files that don't normally change together, suggesting cross-cutting concerns that may be harder to test.
pattern_risk.change_locality_down_rec: Review whether these cross-cutting changes have adequate test coverage.
pattern_risk.run_duration_up_impact: Builds are taking longer. Slower CI feedback loops reduce developer productivity and delay catching issues.
pattern_risk.run_duration_up_rec: Profile the build pipeline to identify bottlenecks. Check for newly added expensive tests or build steps.
pattern_risk.run_duration_down_impact: Builds are running faster than usual. Verify this isn't due to skipped tests or simplified steps.
pattern_risk.run_duration_down_rec: Confirm test coverage hasn't decreased alongside faster builds.
pattern_risk.run_failed_up_impact: Build failures are increasing. Broken builds block the team and indicate instability.
pattern_risk.run_failed_up_rec: Investigate immediately. Check recent changes for breaking commits and flaky tests.
pattern_risk.run_failed_down_impact: Build stability is improving. Fewer failures means better developer experience.
pattern_risk.run_failed_down_rec: No action needed. This is a positive trend.
pattern_risk.dependency_count_up_impact: The dependency count is growing, expanding the supply-chain attack surface and potential for version conflicts.
pattern_risk.dependency_count_up_rec: Audit new dependencies for necessity, maintenance status, and known vulnerabilities.
pattern_risk.dependency_count_down_impact: Dependencies are being reduced, shrinking the attack surface and simplifying maintenance.
pattern_risk.dependency_count_down_rec: No action needed. Verify removed dependencies weren't still needed.
pattern_risk.release_cadence_hours_up_impact: Time between releases is increasing. This could indicate a bottleneck in the release process or accumulating risk in larger releases.
pattern_risk.release_cadence_hours_up_rec: Check if process changes or staffing issues are delaying releases.
pattern_risk.release_cadence_hours_down_impact: Releases are happening more frequently. Faster releases reduce batch size risk but may skip review steps.
pattern_risk.release_cadence_hours_down_rec: Verify that quality gates (testing, review) are still being applied to faster releases.
pattern_risk.fallback_strong_impact: A strong statistical correlation exists between these signals. Changes in one area predictably affect the other.
pattern_risk.fallback_strong_rec: Monitor this relationship. If one signal degrades, check the correlated area.
pattern_risk.fallback_moderate_impact: A moderate correlation exists between these signals, suggesting they tend to move together.
pattern_risk.fallback_moderate_rec: Be aware of this relationship when making changes in either area.
pattern_risk.fallback_weak_impact: A weak but recurring correlation exists between these signals.
pattern_risk.fallback_weak_rec: No action needed. This is a background trend to be aware of.

js.command_copied: Command copied — paste in terminal to accept
